classifications.phrase: committed to doing what it can to support the creation of jobs
recent economic data have been a bit better than expected
recovery is still expected to be bumpy and drawn out
positive GDP growth is now expected in the September quarter
unemployment rate is expected to remain high
subdued increases in wages and prices over coming years
Board views addressing the high rate of unemployment as an important national priority
will not increase the cash rate until actual inflation is sustainably within the 2 to…
Board is not expecting to increase the cash rate for at least three years
Board is prepared to do more if necessary

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a dovish stance focused on supporting employment and managing low inflation expectations, with explicit forward guidance ruling out rate increases for at least three years.
